channel.title: Follow Builders — X/Twitter
channel.description: AI builders' latest tweets, curated by follow-builders

item.title: Andrej Karpathy (@karpathy): - Drafted a blog post - Used an LLM to meticulously improve the argument over 4 hours. - Wow, feelin...
item.description:
- Drafted a blog post - Used an LLM to meticulously improve the argument over 4 hours. - Wow, feeling great, it’s so convincing! - Fun idea let’s ask it to argue the opposite. - LLM demolishes the entire argument and convinces me that the opposite is in fact true. - lol
The LLMs may elicit an opinion when asked but are extremely competent in arguing almost any direction. This is actually super useful as a tool for forming your own opinions, just make sure to ask different directions and be careful with the sycophancy.
Bio: I like to train large deep neural nets. Previously Director of AI @ Tesla, founding team @ OpenAI, PhD @ Stanford. ❤️ 23516 🔁 1786

item.title:
Swyx (@swyx): hi it’s me your friendly neighborhood sumo orange devrel.
these are SOTA Oranges. eating 2 of these...
item.description:
hi it’s me your friendly neighborhood sumo orange devrel.
these are SOTA Oranges. eating 2 of these every day will ~fill your daily vitamin C and fiber needs (!!!)
they are delicious, insanely easy to peel (I am dead serious, this will make you revisit what you think an orange eating experience should be, all other oranges are ruined after eating this one) and you can subscribe weekly on Amazon Fresh for like $8.
Bio: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
affiliations: - @dxtipshq - @cognition - @temporalio - @aidotengineer - @latentspacepod ❤️ 104 🔁 1

item.title: Swyx (@swyx): False equivalence is a trap I’ve been consciously steering away from in my work the last 5 years. in...
item.description:
False equivalence is a trap I’ve been consciously steering away from in my work the last 5 years. in Zuck’s case it cost him @GoogleDeepMind.
in content/strategy it is common to go wide than deep: “oh we do a, b, c, and d” and put equal weight on all of them. But the world is not fair and power laws compound. Most school systems, bureaucrats, managers, and content curators are not set up for one thing to matter 50x more than the next thing. False equivalence killed my first devtools startup. False equivalence plagues policy making in my home country. False equivalence makes you underpay your top performers and spend too much time on lost causes.
Rules:
Carefully bet on a very small set of things. Don’t hedge, but keep reversibility. Set triggers/levels to monitor if you are wrong. Set tests to DOUBLE DOWN EARLY if you are more right than you thought.
Bio: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
affiliations: - @dxtipshq - @cognition - @temporalio - @aidotengineer - @latentspacepod ❤️ 255 🔁 21

item.title:
Peter Yang (@petergyang): X asking me to thumbs down my own reply 🥲.
This button should be visible for original posts too no...
item.description:
X asking me to thumbs down my own reply 🥲.
This button should be visible for original posts too not just replies. I see alot of AI slop in my feed still. https://t.co/3y0KhuI0R1
Bio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox ❤️ 61 🔁 1

item.title: Peter Yang (@petergyang): Interesting to see how many fast growing companies like @linear and @tryramp want to hire ex-founder...
item.description:
Interesting to see how many fast growing companies like @linear and @tryramp want to hire ex-founders as PMs.
I think this is a good litmus test for how good your company is at attracting talent:
1. Do founders want to work here?
2. Do they have the agency to make real impact or will they get lost in your org?
Bio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox ❤️ 73 🔁 3

item.title: Amjad Masad (@amasad): AI enables everyone to build apps, which leaves the best engineers to focus a layer deeper and do mo...
item.description:
AI enables everyone to build apps, which leaves the best engineers to focus a layer deeper and do more ambitious things to advance the platforms themselves and expand what’s possible.
This is what I meant when I started talking about “the 1000x engineer” four years ago. https://t.co/YxIHBRUSUJ https://t.co/76kBBvhilQ
Bio: ceo @replit. civilizationist ❤️ 365 🔁 23

item.title:
Garry Tan (@garrytan): After Richmond disabled its Flock cameras to "protect immigrants," car thefts jumped 33%.
Immigran...
item.description:
After Richmond disabled its Flock cameras to "protect immigrants," car thefts jumped 33%.
Immigrant shopkeepers begged to bring them back. Last week their city council finally listened.
Opposing safety cameras is a luxury belief.
https://t.co/4BecRL4WTL
Bio: President & CEO @ycombinator —Founder @garryslist—Creator of GStack—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop ❤️ 935 🔁 89

item.title:
Zara Zhang (@zarazhangrui): A good agent product should be able to do things that its creator did not think it could do
For int...
item.description:
A good agent product should be able to do things that its creator did not think it could do
For internet-era products, you design all the functionalities and a "good product" works according to your expectations
For agent products, you unleash it and it surprises & delights you with things you didn't think were possible
Bio: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w ❤️ 13 🔁 0

item.title:
Peter Steinberger (@steipete): MCPorter (MCP->CLI)🧳0.8.0 is out.
- stronger OAuth handling for servers - valid JSON output on f...
item.description:
MCPorter (MCP->CLI)🧳0.8.0 is out.
- stronger OAuth handling for servers - valid JSON output on fallback paths - better mcporter call behavior and error handling - generated CLIs handle object-valued args better - keep-alive/daemon reliability https://t.co/Nu9UOBxPam
Bio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world @openclaw🦞 ❤️ 543 🔁 41

item.title: Peter Steinberger (@steipete): So much of this, every day. You really have to develop thick skin (exoskeleton?) when working on suc...
item.description:
So much of this, every day. You really have to develop thick skin (exoskeleton?) when working on successful open source.
(The Chrome extension has been removed since Google added native access in 144+, which is simpler, but yes, it does require a one-time setting change) https://t.co/N62y2XfaTW
Bio: Polyagentmorous ClawFather. Came back from retirement to mess with AI and help a lobster take over the world @openclaw🦞 ❤️ 546 🔁 20
